channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: Italy Summons Russian Ambassador Over TV Host Insults to PM Meloni
item.description: Italy summoned the Russian ambassador to protest insults directed at Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni by a pro-Kremlin TV host, escalating diplomatic tensions between Rome and Moscow.

item.title: China Leverages Energy Aid Against Philippines During US Military Drills
item.description: China implied energy assistance for the Philippines could be tied to Manila holding military drills with the US and allies, signaling Beijings willingness to weaponize energy reserves in diplomatic disputes.

item.title: Iran War May Crush Oil Demand Now But Spike It Long-Term
item.description: While the Strait of Hormuz closure is destroying oil demand daily, the longer-term impact of the Iran war may paradoxically boost oil consumption through energy security concerns and a less efficient global energy system.

item.title: South Korea and Vietnam Leaders Meet as Hanoi Seeks High-Tech Investment
item.description: Vietnams leader hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung for their second meeting in under a year, as Hanoi seeks greater support for its high-tech ambitions from its top foreign investor.

item.title: Cyprus President Calls for EU Playbook on Member Defense
item.description: Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides urged EU leaders to prepare a clear playbook for helping member states under attack, highlighting the blocs need for stronger collective defense mechanisms amid growing global threats.

item.title: Iran sustains covert oil exports through Hormuz despite US blockade
item.description: Iran continues crude exports using dark tanker operations, signal spoofing and ship-to-ship transfers to evade the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: IRGC says at peak readiness to respond to any renewed aggression
item.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps vows to deliver crushing blows to remaining enemy assets in the region as ceasefire talks continue.

item.title: Italy summons Russian ambassador after TV host insults Meloni
item.description: Rome protests after a Russian state TV presenter launched a stream of insults at Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on air, escalating diplomatic tensions.

item.title: Iran accuses Pakistan of double game in US talks mediation
item.description: Iranian state media targets Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir, alleging Islamabad is playing both sides in the US-Iran ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: UK inflation jumps to 3.3% as Iran war drives fuel price spike
item.description: British inflation rose sharply as the Iran war and Hormuz closure cause significant increases in fuel prices and cost of living pressures.

item.title: Chinese Navy Passes by Japanese Islands After Pacific Training
item.description: A Chinese naval group including a destroyer passed through a waterway between Okinawa islands as it returned home after far-seas training, following a Japanese transit through the Taiwan Strait.

item.title: Pope Visits Notorious Prison in Equatorial Guinea
item.description: Pope Leo XIV visited one of Equatorial Guinea's notorious prisons, drawing attention to human rights issues following recent US migrant deportations to the country.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Makes Case for Smaller Fed Balance Sheet
item.description: Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh told a Senate panel he would work to reduce the Fed's bond holdings, a long-term project that would reshape the central bank's approach to monetary policy.

item.title: Iran Fires on Container Ship in Strait of Hormuz After Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Iran paramilitary Revolutionary Guard opened fire on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz, damaging the vessel. The attack comes despite Trump extending the ceasefire, raising tensions as peace talks remain uncertain.

item.title: Russia Has Taken 1,700 Square Kilometers of Ukraine This Year, Top General Says
item.description: Russian forces have captured 1,700 sq km of Ukrainian territory in 2026 and are advancing on the Donbas fortress belt. The gains come as Ukraine struggles to secure EU loan funding amid the Druzhba pipeline dispute.

item.title: NATO Criticises Russian and Chinese Nuclear Policies, Urges US Cooperation
item.description: NATO criticized Russian and Chinese nuclear arms policies and called on both countries to work with the United States to establish greater stability and transparency at an upcoming international conference.

item.title: US Indo-Pacific Commander Warns Taiwan on Defense Budget Delay
item.description: The head of US Indo-Pacific Command stressed Taiwan must pass its stalled defense budget, saying the US "cant want Taiwans defense more than they want it themselves" amid rising Chinese military activity.

item.title: Dutch Intelligence: Europe Must Focus on Own Security as Global Threats Mount
item.description: The Dutch MIVD warned Europe must take greater responsibility for its own security, citing pressure on Western alliances and Chinas support for Russias war in Ukraine. The call comes amid US-Iran tensions straining transatlantic ties.

item.title: China may expand role in Saudi ties after Iran war concludes
item.description: SCMP analysis examines how Riyadh may shift toward self-reliance and diversifying partnerships as the Iran conflict reshapes regional dynamics.

item.title: Pakistan blames US blockade, not Iranian divisions, for stalled peace talks
item.description: Nikkei Asia reports Pakistani officials attribute the deadlock in ceasefire negotiations to the US naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Ukrainian drones strike Samara Oblast in overnight attacks
item.description: Reports and social media footage show damage to residential buildings in Russia's Samara region as Ukrainian drones continue targeting Russian territory.

item.title: IEA chief warns Iran war creating worst energy crisis in history
item.description: International Energy Agency head says combined disruptions from Strait of Hormuz closure and Ukraine war fallout are squeezing global oil and gas supplies.

item.title: US halts dollar shipments to Iraq to pressure Iran-backed militias
item.description: Trump administration freezes US dollar deliveries to Iraq and suspends security cooperation programs in effort to curb Iranian influence, according to Wall Street Journal.

item.title: Europe Seeks Role in Iran War but Remains on Sidelines
item.description: A British-French plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz would give Europe a role in the conflict, but Tehran and Washington continue to call the shots, leaving European powers struggling for influence.

item.title: Mideast Crises Divide Europe Over Fuel Costs and Israel Policy
item.description: European diplomats meeting in Luxembourg are grappling with rising fuel costs from the Iran conflict and policy divisions over Israel, as the war in Ukraine and Middle East tensions strain EU unity.
